title: The West Built the Cheap China System Evidence Pack
research_question: Can the TWIS article claim that Western capitalism helped build the cheap China production system and that later efforts to restrict or reprice cheap direct buying risk making poorer consumers pay more?
scope: Controlled TWIS article/sandbox pack using one article-draft source, one Reuters duty source, one official European Commission source, one consumer-impact context source, and one platform-use context source. This pack tests whether Evidence Pack v1 can hold a second small article proof trail with news, official EU, consumer-impact, and platform-use context sources. It is not a full publication evidence pack.
notes: Second controlled TWIS article/sandbox Evidence Pack. It uses one article-draft source, one Reuters duty source, one European Commission source, one Guardian/IFS consumer-impact context source, and one Reuters platform-use context source. The Reuters duty source supports the specific EU low-value parcel duty point. The Commission source supports official context on low-value consignments, the 150 euro threshold, and customs reform. The consumer-impact source supports general low-income price-pressure context. The platform-use source supports general context that Shein and Temu appeal to lower-income bargain-seeking shoppers. External evidence for historic outsourcing, corporate motive, UK low-income platform dependence, pass-through pricing, and measured EU-duty consumer impact is still needed before publication use.
